sourceMovie.title: Days of Being Wild
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Days of Being Wild (1990) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lord of War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Days of Being Wild clicked for you, Lord of War (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Purple Hearts
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Days of Being Wild clicked for you, Purple Hearts (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Days of Being Wild clicked for you, Life in a Year (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Days of Being Wild?
faq.answer: Try Lord of War, Purple Hearts, Life in a Year—each shares DNA with Days of Being Wild while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Days of Being Wild?
faq.answer: Days of Being Wild (1990) is best described as Crime, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Days of Being Wild part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "In the Mood for Love Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
